enrichment.summary: Kyle responds to a viewer request about US-Russia tensions, arguing there is no real conflict between the two countries and that they are becoming more similar. He focuses heavily on Donald Trump's alleged financial ties to Russia, his praise of Putin, and concerns about Trump's fitness for office, while also defending Russia's role in Syria as strategically understandable.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: No real tension — the two countries are becoming more alike and just do business
Dangerous and unfit; financially tied to Russia, possibly willing to sell secrets, and erratic about nuclear weapons
Russia is justified in protecting Syria's government; far better than US-style regime change
The US has toppled governments in dozens of countries over 50 years, making it hypocritical to criticize Russia
Fear-mongering propaganda to discourage voting for Hillary Clinton
America and Russia are converging — America toward oligarchy, Russia toward capitalism

transcript.text: Hello, new video request from Sam O.O.
What are your thoughts on the recent increased tension between Russia and the U.S.?
What do you think will happen?
Thanks.
Okay, I don't really watch news that much, so I'll just talk about what I've seen and when I think about it.
It has a lot to do with the presidential election.
In the last debate, Hillary Clinton accused Donald Trump of having ties with Russia.
I guess they found that he has loans from Russia, a lot of loans from Russia, because I've learned all this on the Young Turks videos, loser Donald segments.
Donald Trump has been bankrupt six times in America, so American banks won't loan him money anymore.
So he goes to Russia to borrow money now, and so he's got maybe deals with Putin.
Some people say he's going to sell American secrets to Russia.
I think he even joked about that in one of his speeches.

And a couple, like a month ago, on like the Instagram or something, Ivanka Trump, Donald Trump's daughter, was taking pictures and hanging out on a vacation with Vladimir Putin's wife, or Vladimir Putin's girlfriend.
And so, and then Donald Trump has been saying really good things about Vladimir Putin, like he's a great, strong leader.
And so, you know, Donald Trump wants to turn a kind of America into Russia.
I think a lot of other people, too.

I saw a video about, from Jean-Luc Turks today, the governor of New Hampshire was like on TV saying that he thought Donald Trump was awesome because he'll provide strength and authority to America because Obama, you know, can't enforce any laws.
And so, yeah, so I don't think there's, there's kind of like, maybe it's, maybe we're just kind of coming to light like America and Russia are kind of becoming kind of the same, you know.
America is becoming an oligarchy like Russia and Russia is becoming kind of more of a capitalist, capitalism like America, you know.
Jesse Ventura just signed to do a TV show with Russia Today, you know, RT, Russia Today.
Well, that's owned by Russia.
Like, Jesse Ventura flew to Russia and hung out and met with Vladimir Putin because I guess Russia's, yeah, because he's involved with, I guess.
It's still kind of a communist country and, yeah, and he asked Vladimir Putin if he was going to tell him what, you know, to try to censor him at all.
He said, no, I won't censor you at all.
And he said that the American networks wouldn't censor him at all.
And he's like, isn't that interesting?
You know, Russia is giving you freedom.
So, yeah, I think Russia and America, you know, we're kind of like sister countries, you know.
I mean, if Russia wants to, Russia is trying to prop up Syria and people are saying that we're going to have a war with Syria.
Like, Jill Stein said if we vote Hillary Clinton as president, I think I'm going to have this cut out, sir.
You know what I mean?
If we vote Hillary Clinton as president, we're going to go to war with Syria.
Like, that's all bullshit.
That's her stupid propaganda trying to, you know, find a reason why to vote for her instead of Hillary Clinton.
And, I mean, at least Russia hasn't attacked any countries.
Iraq, I guess, went in and took a chunk of Ukraine, but Ukrainians are already kind of part Russian.
You know, we went and took over two countries, Afghanistan and Iraq.
Like, and all Russia is trying to do is protect Syria.
Syria is a country.
It was having rebels, and the rebels were so strong that it turned into a civil war.
But Russia is just trying to protect the government of Syria, which is what anybody would want to do.

I mean, it's way better than going in and toppling a government of a country like what we've done to Iraq and Afghanistan and 50 other countries over the last 50 years, okay, that we don't even know about.
Like, you know, Guatemala, Granada, Honduras.
All kinds of them, all kinds of them, all of them, dude.
Panama, you keep going.
Chile, Ecuador, all kinds of tampering with people.
So, you know, that's just bullshit.
That's just bullshit, the fear-mongering that politicians are trying to do.
Russia only has one, like, year-round port in the whole, you know, in their whole empire, and that's in Syria.
Every other port that they have is frozen over in the wintertime, you know, and so that's a huge strategic thing for them.
And so just let them protect Syria, you know, and for us to worry about it and get involved is fucking ridiculous.
Our economy is not healthy enough.
We need to focus on our own stuff.
So there is no tension between Russia and America.
I think Russia is, you know, more of an oligarchy than us, and it's not as advanced of a country and stuff.
But, you know, there's not any tension on the other side of the world.
We're not going to fight each other.
We just do business.
But definitely, if we get Donald Trump in, he could do all sorts of stupid, crazy stuff to, you know, like sell a bunch of secrets to Putin,
And then Putin would get in trouble and get kicked out, and then he gets kicked out, and now we have to argue about, you know, all kinds of stuff.
Or he could try to nuke somebody, you know.
I mean, Donald Trump's nuts.
See, he did a meeting with some top, like, generals, you know, who were, I guess, thinking of endorsing or whatever,
And he asked him four times in one hour, why can't we just nuke him?
So, you know, and he even said in a speech, like, yeah, what's wrong?
Saudi Arabia should have nukes.
Saudi Arabia is attacking another country.
And who are kind of like, who don't need, we're their protectors.
So, yeah, he's nuts.
Yeah, there's no problem between America and Russia.
Okay.
